title: This is the most accurate depiction of Evangelion
display_title: This is the most accurate depiction of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: This is the most accurate depiction of Evangelion             Quote Post by Zkh » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:09 pm http://img184.imageshack.us/img184/3186/loltruedi3.jpg This is EXACTLY how I looked when I saw Evangelion, and is probably how most normal people did as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:16 pm Am I the only person who liked the TV ending?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:19 pm Space Penis wrote: Am I the only person who liked the TV ending?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:21 pm An old 4koma but a funny one, also one that's large enough to merit a link instead of a direct image, IMO. (I've seen versions of this that were done vertically, a little more forum friendly).

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:51 pm BrikHaus wrote: Space Penis wrote: Am I the only person who liked the TV ending?

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Mon Apr 23, 2007 6:59 pm Space Penis wrote: Am I the only person who liked the TV ending?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Apr 23, 2007 7:00 pm Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: FUNNY PICS

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Apr 23, 2007 7:20 pm For the record, I didn't dislike all of EoTV. I rather liked the glimpse of the alternate universe in 26. I kind of wish it had been longer. It was a lot more interesting than a bunch of characters standing around talking without moving their mouths. OK Gainax, you had budget problems, I understand, but how expensive can it really be to film the lip flaps, huh?

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Mon Apr 23, 2007 7:27 pm BrikHaus wrote: For the record, I didn't dislike all of EoTV. I rather liked the glimpse of the alternate universe in 26. I kind of wish it had been longer. It was a lot more interesting than a bunch of characters standing around talking without moving their mouths. OK Gainax, you had budget problems, I understand, but how expensive can it really be to film the lip flaps, huh?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Apr 23, 2007 7:28 pm Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: BrikHaus wrote: For the record, I didn't dislike all of EoTV. I rather liked the glimpse of the alternate universe in 26. I kind of wish it had been longer. It was a lot more interesting than a bunch of characters standing around talking without moving their mouths. OK Gainax, you had budget problems, I understand, but how expensive can it really be to film the lip flaps, huh?

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Mon Apr 23, 2007 7:34 pm I liked EoTV. That is all.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Apr 23, 2007 7:37 pm Haha, love both pics. However, I loved both endings to NGE. The more I've thought about them (and seen them), the more I've realized how crucial to one another they are. I used this analogy before, but it's as if someone ripped out the last chapter of a really good book, and stapled the pages together in a way to create two endings that told the same story from different points of view. I still think they're both highly originally and endlessly powerful and fascinating.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Apr 23, 2007 7:52 pm Space Penis wrote: Am I the only person who liked the TV ending?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 23, 2007 8:17 pm If we're going to use the "This is your brain" pics in this kind of context, I ought to make a couple of additional ones...

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Mon Apr 23, 2007 8:34 pm BrikHaus wrote: Two songs? I guess I wasn't moved and don't have a soul because I don't know what you're talking about.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Apr 23, 2007 8:42 pm Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: 'This heady feeling of Freedom' and 'Good or Don't be'. They start as the 'alternate' reality ends and finish at: 'to all the children congratulations.'

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Apr 23, 2007 8:56 pm Your argument would work if those two musical numbers were SONGS and not just MUSIC. ;) Songs = music adapted for singing That's why it always royally pisses me off when people refer the the classical pieces in NGE (or anything) as "songs". Makes me want to... :evil: :evil: :evil: :evil:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 23, 2007 9:46 pm HALLELUJAH AMEN FREUNDE~!

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Mon Apr 23, 2007 10:05 pm Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: 'This heady feeling of Freedom' and 'Good or Don't be'. They start as the 'alternate' reality ends and finish at: 'to all the children congratulations.'

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 24, 2007 4:10 am Shoddy animation? In MY "Evangelion"?
